messages.assistant:
Here’s what I currently “know” about you from your saved profile/log-derived model. Treat this as **provisional**—I can be wrong, out of date, or missing context, and you can correct anything.

## Basics
- **Name:** Buddy Williams  
- **Location:** Smyrna / Atlanta, Georgia  
- **Work:** **Chief AI Innovation Officer** at **Nevo Technologies** (started **Mar 2025**)  
- **Partner:** Casey  
- **Kids:** Emma and Isaac (those two are consistently evidenced; there may be additional children mentioned less consistently)

## What you reliably optimize for (your “attractors”)
- **Family as the anchor.** You repeatedly organize life around being present, warm, and protective with your kids and managing family logistics—especially under stress.
- **Systems-first problem solving.** When things are uncertain, you respond by making plans, frameworks, contingencies, and models (not just vibes).
- **Epistemic clarity / good explanations.** In 2025 especially, you’ve been building and publishing structured thinking about logic, rationality, and AI economics—and teaching some of it directly to your kids.
- **Values/purpose framing (WHY).** You’ve explicitly used purpose/values as an operational decision tool (visible strongly in 2020, still consistent with later behavior).

## What seems to matter most to you
- **Warmth + play + closeness** with your children (a consistent theme across years).
- **Agency and alignment**—building a life and work that feels like an upgrade and matches your values.
- **Trust and boundaries**—especially around your children’s access and consent.
- **Thriving through movement + connection** (you’ve named rock climbing as a “best time” experience; you miss movement/community practices you used to have).

## Your stress responses / protective moves
- When **trust breaks**, you’re willing to enforce **hard boundaries**, even at high relational cost (notably a rupture with your mom in late 2024 related to unauthorized contact with the kids).
- When life gets messy or uncertain, you tend to **model + plan + experiment** rather than surrender to drift.

## Recent (late-2025) wellbeing constraints you’ve named
- **Loneliness / isolation**, reduced community
- **Low movement/exercise**
- **Insomnia / low energy**
- Some **depressive** flavor or “low vitality” concern
- You’ve been running **self-experiments** (including a personal project called **Euno**) to climb out of that state.

## Ideas and influences that show up in your thinking
- Popper (conjecture/refutation), Deutsch (good explanations), Hoffman (fitness/utility framing), Friston/Clark (active inference / predictive processing)
- Interest in AI-driven economic change (you’ve written a lot in 2025 on AI economics / meaning/scarcity dynamics)
- You engage with rationalist/EA/LessWrong ideas as both peers and foils.

## Relationship landscape (high-level)
- **Casey:** co-parent + collaborator; couple time exists but has been constrained.
- **Mom:** warm earlier → major rupture in 2024 → tentative re-engagement in 2025 (with boundaries as a central issue).
- **Extended support:** Lee & Ruth Heffernan have been important supports (including childcare support in 2024).

---

### Quick calibration questions (so I don’t build on bad premises)
1. Are **Emma and Isaac** the only kids I should assume, or are there others you want included explicitly?
2. Do you still want me to treat the **mom-boundary situation** as “active/fragile,” or has it stabilized?
3. If you had to pick one theme for your current season: **career build**, **family stability**, or **personal thriving recovery**—which is most central right now?
